entries.title: Robust CLI + Codebase Atlas explorer + Bug fixes
entries.summary: mushi-mushi CLI v0.7.0 with 8 new commands (whoami, ping, reports resolve/reopen/dismiss/search, lessons list/show), all routed through new /v1/sync/* API-key endpoints — no JWT needed. Codebase Atlas (/explore) visualises your indexed source as a swimlane column graph with layer filtering and semantic search. Three bug fixes: correct @mushi-mushi/mcp package name in setup wizard, accurate project stats counts (no 1000-row cap), and banner install-command respects user-selected framework tab.

highlights.text: CLI v0.7.0 — 8 new commands: `mushi whoami` (verify key + project), `mushi ping` (connectivity check), `mushi reports resolve/reopen/dismiss/search`, `mushi lessons list/show`. Exit codes 0/1/2/3. All commands use /v1/sync/* endpoints — no Supabase JWT required, just your project API key.

New /v1/sync/* server endpoints (apiKeyAuth) — /whoami, /stats, /reports, /reports/:id (GET+PATCH), /lessons/:id, /codebase/upload. Fixes INSUFFICIENT_SCOPE errors that affected status, reports list, and reports show with SDK API keys.

Codebase Atlas (/explore) — ReactFlow swimlane graph of indexed source files coloured by architectural layer (UI, Lib, Backend, Test, Config). Layer-filter pills narrow the view. Semantic search via match_codebase_files embedding RPC. Three view modes: Graph, Layer Sankey, Search.

Setup wizard MCP panel — package name corrected to @mushi-mushi/mcp (was mushi-mcp which doesn't exist on npm). Global install guidance: `npm install -g @mushi-mushi/mcp` or `npx -y @mushi-mushi/mcp@latest`.

Project stats endpoint (/v1/sync/stats) — status and severity counts now use DB-level HEAD count queries instead of client-side row counting, eliminating silent 1 000-row cap on Supabase max_rows. Fix totals also corrected (was reading fixes.length instead of DB count).

Setup wizard SDK banner — install command now matches the user's selected framework tab when auto-detection confidence is < 50%, preventing the banner from showing a React command while the user is on the Vue or Svelte tab.

frameworkDetect shell-safe placeholders — workspace path hints now use your-app (no angle brackets) so copy-pasted install commands don't fail due to shell redirection. Fallback also changed from 'your app workspace' (spaces) to 'your-app'.

entries.title: Closed-loop evolution — Lessons, Releases, Iterate, Drift, Experiments, Anomalies, Rewards
entries.summary: Six phases of the antifragile feedback loop: every bug report is now embedded, clustered into a named learning rule, synced to .mushi/lessons.json, injected into your next PR review and AI agent run, and credited back to the user who found it. Plus SDK v0.11.0 (Hermes-compatible React Native), framework auto-detection in the setup wizard, and copyable Project IDs on the Projects page.

highlights.text: Lessons (/lessons) — BIRCH-style streaming mistake clusterer promotes coherent bug clusters to named learning rules. mushi sync-lessons writes .mushi/lessons.json into your repo automatically. lessons.query MCP tool injects token-budget-ranked rules into PR review and AI agent context.

Releases (/releases) — auto-drafted changelogs with exact reporter attribution. SDK widget shows a toast to the user whose report triggered the fix: 'v1.2.3 shipped — you helped fix the settings back-button'.

Iterate (/iterate) — PDCA producer/critic loop with selectable critique personas (Tufte, NN/g-heuristic, WCAG a11y, mobile-first). Live progress + draft-PR exit. Typical run: 3 iterations, < 3 min, score ≥ 0.7 on dogfood pages.

Drift (/drift) — Stagehand-based contract walker compares live app behaviour against inventory + OpenAPI + DB schema. Findings feed back into the lesson library. Thompson-sampled walk priority revisits drift-prone pages on subsequent cycles.
Experiments (/experiments) — sticky A/B assignments via mushi.experiment(). CUPED + mSPRT sequential analysis with automatic SRM detection. Power-curve calculator prevents under-powered runs.
Anomalies (/anomalies) — STL + Page-Hinkley + isolation-forest detectors on inbound adapter metrics. Confirmed regressions auto-open reports and feed into the lesson library.

Rewards (/rewards) — full reward rules, tiers, quests, webhooks, disputes, and fraud-detection. End users earn points for reports that lead to fixes, with configurable payout rules and a dispute workflow.
Cost (/cost) — LLM spend breakdown across clustering, judging, PDCA, drift, and anomaly pipelines. Every new LLM call is attributed to a pipeline and surfaced per-project.

Setup wizard auto-detects framework and monorepo type from a pasted package.json (React, Vue, Svelte, Angular, React Native, Expo, Capacitor, Vanilla JS; npm / yarn / pnpm workspaces, Turborepo, Nx, Lerna). Shows workspace-scoped install commands automatically.
Project ID is now a copyable chip on the Projects page — no more digging through settings to find MUSHI_PROJECT_ID for the MCP server or CLI.

@mushi-mushi/react-native v0.11.0 — Hermes compatibility fix: replaced new Function() dynamic imports with lazy require() for netinfo and expo-sensors. Enables trigger: 'button' safely on all RN 0.84+ Hermes/AOT builds. Update your dependency to ^0.11.0.
BYOK hardening: all LLM calls now route through resolveLlmKey() from _shared/byok.ts. OpenAI and Anthropic test probes both return 'Connection OK'. PDCA runner no longer targets localhost.

entries.title: Closing the data pipeline loop — failure categories, fix RAG, drift watch, multi-repo
entries.summary: Every fix attempt now categorises why it failed, the fix-worker learns from past merged fixes, inventory drift auto-proposes updates, structured reporter feedback feeds the prompt-tune loop, and a single bug can fan out fixes across multiple linked repos.

highlights.text: "Why fixes failed" KPI on /fixes — every failed attempt is now bucketed (sandbox_timeout, llm_invalid_json, github_403, scope_blocked, …) with a hover histogram, so the dominant failure mode is one glance away.
Past-fix RAG — every merged PR is embedded into a fix_corpus and the next dispatch retrieves the closest prior fixes as in-context examples for the LLM. Fewer hallucinated diffs, more on-pattern PRs.
Inventory drift watch — an hourly cron compares discovered routes against your accepted inventory.yaml and auto-opens a draft proposal when they diverge. No more stale spec.
Structured reporter feedback — replies on /reports/:id can now carry a confirms / wrong_target / noise signal that the prompt-tune loop ingests as ground truth alongside merge outcomes.
Multi-repo coordination — a fix that touches files outside the primary repo now spawns child dispatch jobs against every linked sibling repo and cross-links the PRs in a coordination thread.
Standard Webhooks push notifications — A2A tasks can subscribe a customer URL and receive webhook-id / webhook-timestamp / webhook-signature events without polling SSE.
Inventory crawler no longer throws "not a valid ByteString" on first crawl — non-ASCII characters are stripped from the User-Agent header to comply with RFC 7230.
Stripe metering cron is back — usage_events now sync to Stripe Meter Events every hour, fixing the silent gap that left billable usage stuck in 'pending' since the last migration wave.
Resolved two production-only crashes in /v1/admin/integrations and idempotency upsert that only surfaced after a stale edge bundle deploy.

entries.title: Sidebar polish, SDK heartbeat, and a hotkey-discoverable inbox
entries.summary: Collapsible chrome, count badges on Notifications/Queue/Health, an SDK connectivity card on every project, the rebuilt Inbox UX, and an end-to-end WCAG-AA contrast pass.

highlights.text: SDK health card on every project — live heartbeat per runtime, version with npm release-notes link, and an out-of-date pill when the project trails the latest minor.
Sidebar count badges for Notifications, Queue, and Health — backlog at a glance from any page.
Sidebar collapses to icons-only with a memorable hotkey, and the user card at the foot now opens a popover with org/project/density/theme controls in one menu.
Inbox rebuilt with a unified action queue, keyboard-first triage (j/k, e to archive, x to select), and an empty state that suggests the next-best action.
Hotkeys modal (?) now searchable; every documented shortcut in one filterable list.
Editorial 404 across every wrong URL — branded body, recovery CTA back to the relevant section, base-URL aware so deep-links survive a redirect.
WCAG-AA contrast pass on chrome + card-elevated stat tiles (the dark-mode tiles no longer leak into light mode).
Sentry cluster + living-config help in Settings now opens deep-linked to the relevant config row.

entries.title: Mushi v2 — bidirectional inventory + agentic-failure gates
entries.summary: A declarative inventory.yaml of stories, pages, and actions, five composite gates, a synthetic monitor, and a new Surface mode in the graph that overlays the positive inventory on the live bug map.

highlights.text: User stories · Inventory page (sidebar → User stories) — per-story cards with verified / unwired / regressed counts and a one-click Run gates / Run crawler from the row.
Discovery tab — the SDK proposes the inventory. Turn on capture.discoverInventory and Claude drafts an inventory.yaml from the production stream every 24 h.
Five composite gates rolled into one GitHub check: no-dead-handler, no-mock-leak, inventory drift, agentic-failure detection, and synthetic walk health.
Synthetic monitor re-walks expected_outcome against staging on a cron — fail-closed by default, with explicit synthetic_monitor_allow_mutations opt-in for write paths.
Knowledge graph gains a Surface mode — overlay every Page / Element / Action from inventory.yaml on the live bug graph so the dead corners light up.

@mushi-mushi/mcp-ci ships gates / propose / discover-api / discovery-status / auth-bootstrap commands; the same surface is exposed as MCP tools so Cursor / Claude Code / Copilot can run them on your behalf.

entries.title: Billing clarity + support tickets you can talk to
entries.summary: Quota severity, complimentary admin accounts, plan-aware sparkline trends, a clickable support ticket history with admin replies, and inline create flows for projects and teams in the global header.

highlights.text: Billing usage bar now flips ok / warn / danger as you approach quota, with an 80% milestone tick and a plain-English headline (e.g. 'You're 47 reports over this month — overages bill at $0.005 / report').
Last-30-days sparkline on each project card shows daily reports at a glance, with totals, peak day, and 'last active' caption.
Plans-at-a-glance table now annotates each tier with how your current usage fits ('✓ Fits', '⚠ Tight', '✕ Over').

Complimentary 'admin' org mode for internal accounts — header pill, plan badge, and billing card all read 'Admin' with the underlying tier as secondary context, and Stripe CTAs (checkout, invoices) are silenced.
Support tickets are now clickable: open any row to read your message, see the operator's reply inline, and cancel an open ticket if you've already solved it.
ProjectSwitcher and OrgSwitcher dropdowns each gained a footer '+ New project' / '+ New team' affordance — no more side trips to /projects or /organization to spin up a new workspace.
Hardened CORS for /v1/support/* and /v1/super-admin/*, fixing the 'TypeError: Failed to fetch' that blocked support ticket submission from kensaur.us/mushi-mushi.
Password recovery deep-link no longer bounces users back to the sign-in page; reset flow detects the recovery token synchronously.
Stripe 'No such customer' invoice errors no longer surface for complimentary orgs — invoices route returns an empty list with a helpful note instead of throwing.

entries.title: Live-everywhere overhaul
entries.summary: Realtime on every list page, saved views, sparkline KPIs, and a light/dark/system theme.

highlights.text: Reports, Fixes, Repo, and Dashboard now hydrate via Supabase realtime — no more 5–15s polling delay.
Activity drawer (top bar bell) streams every fix_event as it lands, with an unread badge.
Quick filter chips on Reports (All · New · Triaged · Resolved · Critical) with live counts.
Saved views on Reports — name your current filter set and recall it with one click.
Spacebar preview drawer on Reports: peek a row without losing list scroll.
Sparklines on every StatCard so trends show up at a glance.
Sidebar health dots on Reports / Fixes / Repo surface backlog, failures, and open PRs in real time.
Optimistic dispatch rows on Fixes — retries appear in-flight within a frame.
Breadcrumbs on detail pages and a DataTable primitive (TanStack) migrated into Audit.
Density (compact/default/comfortable) and theme (dark/light/auto) toggles in the sidebar footer.
Keyboard cheatsheet modal (press ?) and Cmd+J Ask Mushi sidebar scoped to the current page.
